machinery_genes.note: Perna et al 1998 Table 2 of the complete EHEC O157:H7 (EDL933) LEE sequence lists escC (formerly sepC) as 'Type III secretion apparatus' (L0041, 512 aa). SctC secretin role from the T3SS apparatus brief Esc/Sct mapping. Sakai LEE is >99% identical to EDL933, same O157:H7 serovar; the apparatus is chromosomal LEE-encoded (selC), not on plasmid pO157, but it is the machinery that translocates this instance's plasmid-encoded effector context.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0039, 190 aa); escJ formerly designated sepD in this paper. SctJ inner-membrane ring role from brief mapping.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0024, 406 aa). SctD MS-ring role from brief mapping.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0049, 217 aa); formerly sepI. SctR export-gate role from brief mapping.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0048, 89 aa, 100% identical between strains); formerly sepH. SctS export-gate role from brief mapping.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0047, 258 aa); formerly sepG. SctT export-gate role from brief mapping.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0046, 345 aa); formerly sepF. SctU autoprotease/switch role from brief mapping.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0035, 675 aa); formerly sepA. SctV major export-ring role from brief mapping.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0034, 446 aa); formerly sepB. SctN ATPase role from brief mapping; EscN biochemically characterized as the EPEC/EHEC T3SS ATPase in later work.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0031, 305 aa, EscQ ortholog). SctQ cytoplasmic C-ring/sorting-platform role from brief mapping; later renamed EscQ in unified Esc nomenclature.
Perna et al 1998 Table 2 (L0018, 73 aa, 100% identical between strains). SctF needle-subunit role from brief mapping; EscF later shown to be the EPEC/EHEC T3SS needle protein.

notes: System: the T3SS (injectisome) that translocates the attaching-and-effacing effectors of E. coli O157:H7. NOTE ON LOCALIZATION: this instance is labelled pO157 (plasmid NC_002128.1), but EHEC O157:H7 has NO complete T3SS injectisome on the pO157 plasmid. The injectisome apparatus is encoded by the chromosomal LEE (Locus of Enterocyte Effacement, integrated at selC). The plasmid-encoded effector context (pO157_p65) is secreted by this LEE T3SS. Per the separated-paper / system-family rule, the apparatus is documented from the founding EHEC O157:H7 LEE sequence paper (Perna et al 1998, EDL933 strain), whose Table 2 labels each of these genes verbatim as 'Type III secretion apparatus.' Sakai's LEE is >99% identical to EDL933's and of the same serovar, so the gene set transfers directly. Roles/Sct-family assignments taken from the T3SS apparatus brief Esc<->Sct mapping. COVERAGE: secretin (EscC), inner/MS rings (EscJ, EscD), all export-gate genes (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscV), ATPase (EscN), C-ring/sorting platform (SepQ/EscQ), needle (EscF) = core injectisome documented gene-by-gene from one paper, hence COMPLETE. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: (1) SepZ -- although Perna 1998 Table 2 labelled it 'Type III secretion apparatus', it was subsequently reclassified as the translocated EFFECTOR EspZ (Kanack et al 2005, PMID 15972527), so it is excluded per the no-effectors rule. (2) SepL/SepD -- gatekeeper/secretion-switch proteins (SctW family); the literature I found names them as regulators of the secretion hierarchy rather than structural apparatus components, so excluded to avoid an indefensible call (would be the natural next additions if a structural-naming quote were required). (3) Translocon proteins EspA/EspB/EspD and chaperones (CesD etc.), intimin (Eae), Tir, and the Ler/GrlA regulators -- excluded as translocator/effector/chaperone/regulator, not core apparatus. (4) EscI inner rod and EscP ruler not separately named as apparatus in Perna 1998 Table 2, so not listed. The pO157 plasmid itself encodes a partial/separate ETT2-like or no injectisome; not used here. Did NOT mine NC_002128.1.
